source: All-In Podcast — E274: SpaceX $2T case, Nvidia shock selloff
summary: GPU useful lives of 10-15 years (enabled by disaggregated inference with domain-specific accelerators) validate neo-cloud amortization schedules and financing structures
summary_extended: Baker argues that the debate over GPU useful lives has been resolved in favor of the bulls: by disaggregating inference workloads—routing decode tasks to domain-specific accelerators like Groq or Cerebras chips in front of older GPUs—those older GPUs retain economic utility for 10 to 15 years rather than the 2-year useful life that bears had assumed. This structural shift directly validates the amortization schedules (4-6 years) and asset-backed financing rates (~6%) used by neo-cloud operators like CoreWeave, which had been attacked as financially unsound. Baker credits Nvidia's Q1 results as the catalyst that 'single-handedly saved the neo-clouds' by demonstrating sustained demand and proving the economics of the disaggregated inference model. The investment implication is that neo-cloud operators with long-duration GPU financing structures are vindicated, and their equity and debt valuations should reflect longer asset lives and more durable cash flows. Investors should watch CoreWeave and similar neo-cloud operators as direct beneficiaries of this re-rating in GPU asset economics.
detail: Baker argues that by disaggregating inference—using Groq or Cerebras accelerators for decode in front of older GPUs—older GPUs have useful lives of 10-15 years, not 2 years as bears argued. This validates CoreWeave's 4-6 year amortization and ~6% asset-backed financing rates. He says Nvidia's Q1 quarter 'single-handedly saved the neo-clouds' by proving this.
confidence_evidence: Baker states this as a resolved debate: 'That quarter single-handedly saved the neo-clouds'
quote_fragment: those older GPUs have a useful life for 10 or 15 years

ticker_audit.reasoning: CoreWeave is explicitly named as the neo-cloud operator whose 4-6 year amortization schedules and ~6% asset-backed financing rates are validated by the longer GPU useful life thesis. It is the primary direct subject of the investment implication.

Baker explicitly credits Nvidia's Q1 quarter as the catalyst that validated neo-cloud economics, and longer GPU useful lives sustain demand for Nvidia's GPU ecosystem. Nvidia is a direct beneficiary of the thesis being proven correct, though it is not the primary subject.
